items.title: Engineering & Exploring the Red Planet • Anita Sengupta & Kamal Oudrhiri • YOW! 2015
items.author: GOTO Conferences
items.content:
<article>This presentation was recorded at YOW! 2017. #GOTOcon #YOW
https://yowcon.com

Anita Sengupta - Research Professor at University of Southern California @spacegirl2020 
Kamal Oudrhiri - 

RESOURCES
Anita
https://bsky.app/profile/dranitasengupta.bsky.social
https://anitasengupta.com
https://twitter.com/Doctor_Astro
https://linkedin.com/in/anitasen

Kamal
https://coldatomlab.jpl.nasa.gov/who-we-are/kamal-oudrhiri
https://x.com/KOudrhiri
https://www.linkedin.com/in/kamal-oudrhiri-221175233

ABSTRACT
On August 5th 2012, NASA landed its most capable robotic geologist on the surface of the Red Planet. The Mars Science Laboratory (MSL) mission landed a 2000 lb rover, the size of a compact car, to explore the planes of Mars. The rover, aptly named Curiosity, will search for organic compounds, characterize the climate and geology, and continue the search for life.

One of the most challenging aspects of the mission, from an engineering perspective, was safely landing the rover on the surface. The entry descent and landing (EDL) system used a heat shield to accommodate its hypersonic entry conditions, followed by a supersonic parachute, and eight retro rockets for the powered descent phase. For its final terminal descent, a maneuver called the sky crane was used where the rover was lowered on tethers for touchdown.

The talk will describe the motivation for Mars Exploration and how the MSL EDL engineering challenges were tackled with computational modeling and cutting edge experimental techniques.

RECOMMENDED BOOKS
NASA • NASA Systems Engineering Handbook • https://amzn.to/3FWjqhR
Bernice Kastner • Space Mathematics • https://amzn.to/3oQCDKF
Kellie Gerardi • Not Necessarily Rocket Science • https://amzn.to/3CW4HS9
Piers Bizony & Roger D. Launius • NASA Space Shuttle: 40th Anniversary • https://amzn.to/3xgsNWh

https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto-

items.title: Someone finally did it: A private AI Keyboard (CM5 Roundup)
items.author: Jeff Geerling
items.content:
<article>Wow there's a lot to cover, from the tiniest Pi computer to a full Pi AI mechanical keyboard!

Sam has listed his prototype TypeOrTalk V2 keyboard on eBay, if you want to bid on it: https://www.ebay.com/itm/267259133332

Here are _all_ the projects I covered today—the Amazon links are affiliate links:

  - Sentinel Core: https://sanctuary-systems.com/sentinel-core/

items.title: The AI keyboard: TypeOrTalk
items.author: Jeff Geerling
items.content:
<article>The TypeOrTalk is a local, private, offline AI-powered keyboard that translates your speech into text. And it's a fine custom mechanical keyboard, too!

If you want to get notified of any updates, check out: https://typeortalk.com
And Sam's auctioning off a prototype on eBay! Check it out here: https://www.ebay.com/itm/267259133332

Sam paid me nothing for this video, and I'm shipping him back the keyboard now that I'm finished testing it. He and I have been talking (and typing, lol) about this project for a while, and I wanted to show off his project since I thought it was a neat idea and an inspirational project for anyone thinking a project like this is out of reach of an individual!

What do you think? Also check out the long form video going over this and a bunch of other CM5 hardware projects!

Support me on Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/geerlingguy
Sponsor me on GitHub: https://github.com/sponsors/geerlingguy
Merch: https://www.redshirtjeff.com
2nd Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@GeerlingEngineering
3rd Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@Level2Jeff</article>

items.title: From XP to TCR & Limbo • Kent Beck & Daniel Terhorst-North
items.author: GOTO Conferences
items.content:
<article>This interview was recorded for GOTO Unscripted.
https://gotopia.tech

Read the full transcription of this interview here
 (https://gotopia.tech/articles/355) 
Kent Beck - Software Engineer & Creator of Extreme Programming
Daniel Terhorst-North - Originator of Behavior Driven Development (BDD) & Principal at Dan North & Associates

RESOURCES
Kent
https://bsky.app/profile/kentbeck.bsky.social
https://www.kentbeck.com
https://github.com/KentBeck
https://twitter.com/KentBeck
https://www.linkedin.com/in/kentbeck

Daniel
https://bsky.app/profile/tastapod.com
https://twitter.com/tastapod
https://www.linkedin.com/in/tastapod
https://github.com/tastapod
https://mastodon.social/@tastapod
http://dannorth.net/blog

DESCRIPTION
Kent Beck and Daniel Terhorst-North reflect on the evolution of Extreme Programming (XP) and its lasting impact on agile software development. They explore the importance of short feedback loops, iterative learning, and adaptability in different phases of product development, as outlined in Beck’s 3X model (Exploration, Expansion, Extraction).

Kent introduces radical techniques like Test && Commit || Revert (TCR) and Limbo, which challenge traditional coding and collaboration practices by emphasizing tiny, safe iterations. The conversation ties in psychological safety, a key factor in high-performing teams, as outlined by Amy C. Edmondson.

Kent envisions a future where software development scales in real-time, pushing teams to embrace faster experimentation, responsibility, and continuous learning in a rapidly evolving tech landscape.

RECOMMENDED BOOKS
Kent Beck • Tidy First?
 (https://amzn.to/4gscjjK) Kent Beck & Cynthia Andres • Extreme Programming Explained
 (https://amzn.to/3sBASDG) Amy C. Edmondson • The Fearless Organization
 (https://amzn.to/3Q5QPxu) Amy C. Edmondson • Right Kind of Wrong
 (https://amzn.to/3CRbiTD) Jez Humble & David Farley • Continuous Delivery (https://amzn.to/452ZRky)

items.title: ChatLoopBackOff Episode 58 (​Helm)
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>A CNCF graduated project since 2020, Helm is an open source package manager for Kubernetes. It provides the ability to provide, share, and use software on clusters. It is a highly customizable tool, and it abstracts the application deployment to a simple configuration file.

Originally created in 2015, Helm is driving into 2025 with plans of a major new version, Helm 4, which has brought in new contributors and users who are passionate about application deployment on Kubernetes.

Join CNCF ambassador Satyam Soni as he explores Helm for the first time.</article>

items.title: Serverless Apps on Cloudflare • Ashley Peacock & Ricky Robinett • GOTO 2025
items.author: GOTO Conferences
items.content:
<article>This interview was recorded for the GOTO Book Club. #GOTOcon #GOTObookclub
http://gotopia.tech/bookclub

Read the full transcription of the interview here:
https://gotopia.tech/episodes/379

Ashley Peacock - Staff Software Engineer at Simply Business & Author of "Serverless Apps on Cloudflare" & "Creating Software with Modern Diagramming Techniques" @ashleypeacock3928 
Ricky Robinett - VP Developer Relations & Community at Cloudflare @rickyrobinett 

RESOURCES
Ashley
https://twitter.com/_ashleypeacock
https://www.linkedin.com/in/ashley-peacock-133749120
https://medium.com/@ashley-peacock
https://github.com/apeacock1991

Ricky
https://twitter.com/rickyrobinett
https://www.linkedin.com/in/rickyrobinett
https://about.me/rickyrobinett
https://github.com/rickyrobinett

Links
https://www.cursor.com
https://youtu.be/3a0WHZVb1Gg
https://youtu.be/_KvFapRkR9I
https://youtu.be/tn6nkZP1iSY
https://youtu.be/X6fPkRON3X8
https://youtu.be/esMjP-7jlRE
https://youtu.be/vIGIQZmwcqo

DESCRIPTION
Ricky Robinett interviews Ashley Peacock, author of "Serverless Apps on Cloudflare", about the developer platform side of Cloudflare. Ashley explains how Cloudflare has evolved from primarily a security company to a full-fledged developer platform with global deployment capabilities, databases, caching solutions, and AI tools.

They discuss the unique aspects of Cloudflare's architecture, including global deployment by default, bindings that simplify resource connections without requiring secrets management, and excellent local development experience.

Ashley highlights several Cloudflare products including Workers (serverless functions), D1 (SQLite database), KV (key-value store), R2 (object storage), Durable Objects, and AI offerings like Workers AI and AI Gateway. The conversation covers developer experience, using AI assistants for coding, and the benefits of Cloudflare's approach to simplifying cloud development.

TIMECODES
00:00 Intro
01:47 From CDN to platform: Cloudflare’s serverless story
05:41 Diving into Cloudflare technologies
12:01 DevEx & AI integration
21:31 Local development
24:15 Data & storage
29:18 Vectorize, Workers AI & AI Gateway
36:42 Closing thoughts
38:26 Outro

items.title: Serverless Compute at the Heart of Your EDA • Julian Wood • GOTO 2024
items.author: GOTO Conferences
items.content:
<article>This presentation was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen 2024. #GOTOcon #GOTOcph
https://gotocph.com

Julian Wood - Serverless Developer Advocate at AWS @julian_wood 

RESOURCES
https://bsky.app/profile/julianwood.com
https://twitter.com/julian_wood
http://www.wooditwork.com
https://www.linkedin.com/in/julianrwood
https://s12d.com/goto

Links
https://s12d.com/goto
https://serverlessland.com

ABSTRACT
Many event-driven applications require business logic to run in response to your events. Deciding how best to integrate various compute services can be a challenge.

This session shows how to use both serverless functions and serverless containers effectively to handle your EDA compute needs. Using a serverless approach allows you to spend more time solving business requirements, rather than dealing with infrastructure management.

Learn how both AWS Lambda and Amazon ECS on AWS Fargate scale up to meet demand when you need it and how you can scale to zero, even with containers. Discover how you can use both compute services in the same application, using smart orchestration to choose the best service for the job. [...]

TIMECODES
00:00 Intro
01:57 Serverless EDA
05:42 Serverless compute
12:43 Demo
16:02 Serverless functions in action
17:43 Demo
20:20 Amazon ECS on AWS Fargate
31:08 EDA patterns using containers
37:46 Demo
39:18 Which serverless compute option is right for my app?
46:32 The road ahead
46:58 Outro

Download slides and read the full abstract here:
https://gotocph.com/2024/sessions/3318

RECOMMENDED BOOKS

https://joshlong.com
https://www.biodrop.io/joshlong
https://github.com/gunnarmorling/1brc

Links
https://start.spring.io
https://github.com/joshlong-attic/2025-05-13-goto-state-of-the-art-series

ABSTRACT
Josh Long demonstrates how Spring AI (GA as of May 2025) enables Java developers to build production-ready AI applications with ease. He addresses common AI challenges including lack of direction, statelessness, inability to interact with external systems, and hallucination through features like system prompts, chat memory, tool calling, and retrieval-augmented generation. The demonstration builds a complete dog adoption application with vector stores, chat memory, and tool calling capabilities, showcasing how Java can be a superior choice for AI workloads compared to other languages.

Josh let's you explore the latest-and-greatest in Spring AI to build faster, more scalable, more efficient, more modular, more secure, and more intelligent systems and services. [...]

items.title: How to Lead Your Organisation’s AI-Transformation • Rasmus Lystrøm • YOW! 2024
items.author: GOTO Conferences
items.content:
<article>This presentation was recorded at YOW! Australia 2024. #GOTOcon #YOW
https://yowcon.com

Rasmus Lystrøm - International Speaker and Chief Disruptor @ondfisk666 

ORIGINAL TALK TITLE
How to Lead your Organization's Al-Transformation: Strategies, Skills & Culture

RESOURCES
https://bsky.app/profile/ondfisk.bsky.social
https://x.com/ondfisk
https://www.linkedin.com/in/rasmuslystroem
https://github.com/ondfisk
https://ondfisk.dk

ABSTRACT
Explore the strategic approach and cultural evolution needed for AI transformation in organisations. Highlight the empowerment of teams with essential skills and decision-making power to leverage AI for business impact. Address AI’s role in both creative and routine tasks, and advocate for beginning with straightforward, practical systems before expanding. [...]

TIMECODES
00:00 Intro
04:11 Part 1: GenAI transformation
04:17 What is GenAI?
05:56 Leadership strategy No. 1
08:48 Leadership strategy No. 2
10:41 Leadership strategy No. 3
13:47 Leadership strategy No. 4
20:22 Part 2: AI transformation
20:38 What is AI transformation?
23:24 What is it you want?
32:45 Trust your people
36:06 A final note
38:07 Outro

Download slides and read the full abstract here:
